payload.hypothesis: Start with balanced exploration: CP-SAT for optimality, moderate top-k and bundle generation to handle rider-shortage cases, and LNS for improvement within time budget.
Start with balanced exploration: CP-SAT for optimality, moderate top-k and bundle generation to handle rider-shortage cases, and LNS for improvement within time budget.

payload.summary: Initial baseline achieved 23.7 completed orders with moderate cost, establishing a solid foundation for rider-shortage scenarios.
payload.keep_reason: No incumbent exists, so this result serves as the starting benchmark.
risks: Top-k=2 may miss viable rider assignments in dense areas
Bundle pool size of 8 may limit exploration of bundle combinations
LNS destroy fraction of 0.2 may be too conservative for significant improvements
next_focus: Test higher top-k values to capture more rider options
Increase bundle pool size to explore more bundle combinations
Experiment with more aggressive LNS destruction rates
Validate bundle distance threshold of 2.5 against spatial distribution
avoid_patterns: Over-relying on CP-SAT for large instances without LNS refinement
Keeping bundle generation too restrictive early in search
Using identical parameters across different problem densities

payload.hypothesis: Explore more aggressive bundle generation and rider assignment to address rider-shortage cases. Increase top-k riders to capture more assignment options and expand bundle candidate pool for better bundle exploration while maintaining CP-SAT optimality.

Explore more aggressive bundle generation and rider assignment to address rider-shortage cases. Increase top-k riders to capture more assignment options and expand bundle candidate pool for better bundle exploration while maintaining CP-SAT optimality.

payload.summary: Experiment llm-exp-2 increased completed orders by 2.5% but at a 14% cost increase, suggesting aggressive bundling found more assignments but with less efficiency.
payload.keep_reason: The significant increase in completed orders justifies keeping this configuration for further analysis.
risks: Cost per completed order increased substantially
Bundle generation parameters may be too aggressive on distance/discount
Higher computational time suggests approaching solver limits
next_focus: Optimize bundle distance and discount factors to reduce cost inflation
Test intermediate bundle pool sizes between current and previous
Monitor solver time as complexity increases
avoid_patterns: Sacrificing cost efficiency for marginal order completion gains
Expanding bundle parameters without cost controls
